parsed_review.summary: CP00-495 is a descriptor-only governance-core slice (RP16.P05.M05.S03-RP16.P05.M05.S12, 10 units, risk A, micro-title 'Permission And Audit Binding') and is supported by implementation, tests, and recorded local/Hermes validation. The pack is the live next pack after CP00-494: docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json packs[0] is CP00-495 (unit_count 10), and next-pack-queue.json live_latest_pack_id=CP00-494 with generated preview CP00-495. The manifest, review-baseline, contract (program.current_pack_id, current_pack, cp495_requirements, cp495_no_write_attestation, p05_permission_slice_descriptor, last historical_pack_binding), GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP495 registry constants, validator script, and model.test.js all agree on counts (RP16.P05=10, RP16.P05.M05=10) and deliverable distribution {fixture:4, claude_review:1, implementation:3, security_audit:2}, with the 10 required section rows (Base matter fixture, Base document fixture, Primary/Secondary golden case, Review-required, Denied, Cross-tenant, Missing context, Audit hint, Security trimming). The pack-level production_ready claim is honestly false (status implemented_pending_review, runtime_ready false, implementation_layer descriptor); the production_ready_flag value 'governance_core_p05_permission_slice_descriptor_verified' is a descriptor-verification marker, not a runtime claim, and no production_ready gate is weakened. No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are verified by source inspection: registry.js/service.js/validators.js are pure Object.freeze descriptor builders, and a write/network/exec/db grep across packages/governance/src returned no matches. The validator deep-equals contract<->registry for current_pack, cp495_requirements, cp495_no_write_attestation and the descriptor, asserts descriptor_only=true/runtime_execution=false per row, asserts authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false and no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation, and confirms CP00-495 is the last historical_pack_binding. Claude remains read-only (tools Read/Grep/Glob, read_only true) and not final approver; the human enterprise-trust gate is preserved (enterprise_trust_claim_closed handled outside this receipt). Hermes is used only as a deterministic review-packet/evidence builder (H16.CP00-495 evidence-packet shape; emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false; no runtime embedded in product). Auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1450-byte stdout, valid JSON result ok:true); no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, or tool-call-shaped evidence, and invalid_review_attempts is empty. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) listed in evidence_refs are legitimately pending this review per the documented hardening sequence (command-evidence notes them as post-review gated) and do not block at this stage; the required pre-review artifacts (manifest.json, command-evidence.json) and all review-hardening inputs are present. Changed-file-scope correctly separates the 8 active implementation refs from unrelated dirty files (UI workstream scripts/tools, workbook docs, LDIP review file) with active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty. One non-blocking P3 naming observation is recorded; no P0/P1/P2 issues found.

findings.title: authority_boundary.runtime_opening_pack_id names the next pack (CP00-496) while CP00-495 stays descriptor-only
findings.details: In createGovernanceCoreCp495P05PermissionSliceDescriptor the authority_boundary sets runtime_opening_pack_id to GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP495_PACK_BINDING.next_pack_id (CP00-496), mirrored in contracts/governance-core-contract.json (p05_permission_slice_descriptor.authority_boundary). CP00-495 itself remains descriptor-only (manifest runtime_ready=false; no_*_runtime attestations true; freezeCp495Result flags all dispatch/write/evaluate paths false). The field is always gated by human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true and the closeout_handoff open_scope text ('preserving descriptor-only governance boundaries until downstream packs explicitly open runtime behavior'), so it is a forward-looking candidate marker, not an assertion that CP00-496 opens runtime. The naming could be misread as a commitment that the named pack opens runtime. This is the same by-design forward-reference recorded for CP00-494 (CP494-P3-01).
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout; the human-approval gate and descriptor-only boundary are intact. Optionally clarify the field name (e.g. next_runtime_open_candidate_pack_id) or document the convention so the forward-reference is not misread as a runtime-open commitment.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as by-design forward-reference, consistent across CP00-480..CP00-495; non-blocking. Human approver may note for naming clarity only.
